title: Geospatial Technology and Operation Dronagiri
focus: Components of geospatial technology, Operation Dronagiri, and the National Geospatial Policy 2022.
rationale: This section covers the foundational concepts of geospatial technology and the specific government initiatives like Operation Dronagiri and the National Geospatial Policy aimed at promoting the sector.
sectionExcerpt: Geospatial technology refers to a set of tools and techniques used to collect, analyze, and visualize spatial and geographic data. Components of Geospatial Technology: Geographic Information System (GIS), Global Positioning System (GPS), Remote Sensing (RS), Cartography... Operation Dronagiri: A pilot project under National Geospatial Policy 2022, and will be implemented by Geospatial Innovation Cell, Department of Science and Technology. Purpose: To demonstrate the potential applications of geospatial technologies... National Geospatial Policy 2022: Vision: Position India as global leader in geospatial sector... Nodal Agency: Survey of India (SoI).
evidenceBullets: Geospatial Technology includes GIS, GPS, Remote Sensing (e.g., LiDAR), and Cartography.
Operation Dronagiri is a pilot project under the National Geospatial Policy 2022, implemented by the Geospatial Innovation Cell, Department of Science and Technology.
The first phase of Operation Dronagiri is being implemented in UP, Haryana, Assam, Andhra Pradesh, and Maharashtra.
The National Geospatial Policy 2022 establishes the Geospatial Data Promotion and Development Committee as the apex body.
Survey of India (SoI) is the nodal agency for the National Geospatial Policy and launched the Continuously Operating Reference Stations (CORS) Network.

title: Critical Minerals and Supply Initiatives
focus: Definition and list of critical minerals, international/national initiatives for supply security, and the Ni-Cu-PGE discovery.
rationale: Groups the definition of critical minerals, India's 30-mineral list, strategic initiatives like MSP and KABIL, and the recent domestic discovery of Ni-Cu-PGE sulphide.
sectionExcerpt: The Quad (United States, Japan, India, and Australia) launched the Critical Minerals Initiative. Critical minerals are naturally occurring elements or compounds... Ministry of Mines has released a list of 30 critical minerals... Minerals Security Partnership (MSP): India joined this US-led initiative in 2023. Khanij Bidesh India Limited (KABIL): A joint venture company of Ministry of Mines to acquire critical minerals globally. India's first ever discovery of Nickel-Copper-Platinum Group Element (Ni-Cu-PGE) sulphide at Bhalukona-Jamnidih block of the Mahasamund district in Chhattisgarh.
evidenceBullets: The Quad (US, Japan, India, Australia) launched the Critical Minerals Initiative.
The Ministry of Mines released a list of 30 critical minerals including Lithium, Cobalt, Nickel, and REEs.
India joined the US-led Minerals Security Partnership (MSP) in 2023 to secure critical mineral supply chains.
Khanij Bidesh India Limited (KABIL) is a joint venture by the Ministry of Mines to acquire critical minerals globally.
India's first Ni-Cu-PGE sulphide discovery occurred in the Mahasamund district of Chhattisgarh, which is critical for clean energy and EVs.

title: Rare Earth Elements (REEs)
focus: Characteristics and extraction challenges of Rare Earth Elements.
rationale: A distinct subheading focusing specifically on the properties and geopolitical context of Rare Earth Elements.
sectionExcerpt: In retaliation to US tariffs, China restricts exports of Rare Earth Elements (REEs). What are REEs? REEs are a set of 17 lustrous, silvery-white, soft heavy metals in periodic table, e.g., Cerium (Ce), Yttrium (Y), Europium (Eu), etc. Though found abundantly, however, they are difficult to extract as they are not found in concentrated form.
evidenceBullets: China restricted exports of Rare Earth Elements (REEs) in retaliation to US tariffs.
REEs consist of 17 lustrous, silvery-white, soft heavy metals in the periodic table, such as Cerium, Yttrium, and Europium.
Despite being found abundantly in nature, REEs are difficult to extract because they do not occur in concentrated forms.
